title: Out-of-process compute (R lm over Arrow IPC) — the COMPUTE pillar
description: The COMPUTE pillar as a manifest: a DuckDB table handed to an OUT-OF-PROCESS R computation over Arrow IPC and the result handed back as a table. `points` is a file_scan resource; `lm_fit` is a process.compute resource whose inputSql (SELECT x, y FROM points) is exported as Arrow IPC to the child, which fits an OLS regression (lm — a thing SQL is poor at) and writes the coefficients back as Arrow IPC. NO compute-specific TypeScript: the command, input query, and Arrow contract live in the manifest; the same generic process.compute resolver runs any R/Python/Go/shell tool. The process capability is the host's opt-in by composition (a ProcessRunner must be injected); this manifest fails closed otherwise.

resolvers.title: DuckDB file scan
resolvers.description: Read a delimited file into a table.

resolvers.title: Out-of-process compute
resolvers.description: Run an external process (R/Python/Go/shell) over an Arrow-IPC input and read its Arrow-IPC output back as a table.
